title: Enterprise AI Solutions Sales Specialist
descriptionHtml:
Enterprise AI Solutions Sales Specialist


Salary: £80,000–£100,000 basic | £150,000 OTE (uncapped)
Location: Remote (UK)
Contract: Full‑time, Permanent


About ICS.AI

ICS.AI is a leading UK AI company building next‑generation SMART AI agents through our proprietary conversational AI platform. Our solutions are deployed across websites, intranets, live chat, telephony, email and social channels, delivering automation at scale, improved service outcomes and substantial savings for our customers.


We are also the UK market leader in AI for local government, working with over 20 councils and co‑selling closely with Microsoft to deliver AI Contact Centres, AI Staff Copilots and Social Care Copilots.


We are experiencing rapid growth and are now hiring an Enterprise AI Solutions Sales Specialist to help scale our trial‑based sales process and drive large‑scale AI transformation within local government.


The Opportunity

This role is ideal for a high‑performing seller from SaaS, enterprise technology or advanced solution sales who wants to accelerate their career into the AI sector. You will sell proven, high‑impact AI solutions that are already transforming public services nationwide.


Key Responsibilities

  • Convert marketing‑qualified leads and drive trial‑to‑contract conversion
  • Build and manage a robust enterprise sales pipeline
  • Develop strong relationships with senior stakeholders, including CIOs, Heads of Customer Services and Directors of Social Care
  • Deliver compelling product demonstrations and ROI‑driven proposals
  • Co‑sell with Microsoft and collaborate on joint go‑to‑market strategies
  • Guide prospects through complex, multi‑stakeholder buying cycles across local government

Who You Are

  • A consistent high performer with a strong track record in enterprise or SaaS sales
  • Significant experience selling Contact Centre software or Contact Centre technology solutions (essential)
  • Skilled in influencing senior decision‑makers across public sector or enterprise environments
  • Strong consultative seller able to articulate ROI and business value clearly
  • High‑energy, proactive, target‑driven and commercially astute
  • Technically confident with the ability to translate complex solutions into clear business outcomes
  • Experience selling into local government is advantageous

What We Offer

  • £80,000–£100,000 basic salary
  • £150,000 OTE (uncapped)

Benefits

  • Remote and flexible working
  • 25 days annual leave plus bank holidays
  • Enhanced Pension Scheme
  • Private medical cover (Vitality)
  • £100 per month home‑working allowance
  • Quarterly company team events
  • Full training on AI technologies and our trial‑based sales framework
  • Opportunity to co‑sell with Microsoft
  • Sell market‑leading AI solutions already widely adopted across the UK public sector

Why ICS.AI?

You will be at the forefront of AI adoption across UK public services. This is a high‑impact, high‑growth role, selling cutting‑edge AI solutions with proven outcomes, strong market demand and significant earning potential.


Apply Now

If you are an outstanding sales professional ready to move into enterprise AI and take the next step in your career, we would love to hear from you.

title: Business Development Executive - Dropbox
descriptionHtml:
Business Development Executive - Dropbox

12 Month Fixed Term Contract

About the Job

We’re looking for a driven and commercially minded individual to join our team as a Business Development Executive, responsible for growing and managing Dropbox products and services within our existing IT reseller channel. In this role, you will become a trusted advisor to your accounts, helping them unlock the full value of our cloud solutions by aligning Ingram Micro’s extensive portfolio with their evolving business needs.

This is an exciting opportunity to contribute to the success of a high-growth business unit, while developing a deep understanding of Dropbox technologies and our go-to-market strategy. This is a fixed‑term contract; however, as we continue to grow, there may be future opportunities within the team.

What your role as a Business Development Executive will involve:

  • Proactively develop and expand Dropbox product and service adoption across your customer base

  • Build trusted relationships with partners by aligning Ingram Micro’s value proposition to their business strategy

  • Own and deliver revenue, margin, and trading partner targets within your account portfolio

  • Collaborate with internal stakeholders and vendor peers to create and execute joint business plans

  • Act as the main point of contact between your customers, Ingram Micro, and Dropbox.

  • Support timely financial transactions by coordinating with our finance team and your partners

  • Maintain an up-to-date pipeline of deals and drive them through to close

  • Deliver compelling presentations and contribute to strategic discussions both internally and externally

To set you up for success, we are looking for the following skills and experience

  • Experience in B2B or B2C sales within the IT channel (essential)

  • Background in account management and building long-term client relationships

  • Interest in technology and familiarity with cloud-based services

  • Ability to manage a pipeline of opportunities and see them through to completion

  • Clear and confident communication skills, including the ability to present information to a range of audiences

  • Flexibility and adaptability in responding to customer and vendor needs

  • A tenacious self-starter

We Are Ingram Micro
Ingram Micro is a leading technology company for the global information technology ecosystem. With the ability to reach nearly 90% of the global population, we play a vital role in the worldwide IT sales channel, bringing products and services from technology manufacturers and cloud providers to business-to-business technology experts. Through Ingram Micro Xvantage™, our AI-powered digital platform, we offer what we believe to be the industry’s first business-to- consumer-like experience. We also provide a broad range of technology services, including financing, specialized marketing, and lifecycle management, as well as technical pre- and post- sales professional support. Learn more at www.ingrammicro.com.

Make an application to join the team!
None of this is achievable without great people, with a complete customer focus.

Our team is as much about our people as it is our customers and business partners. We want associates with a strong desire to succeed. We offer a competitive base, commission, market leading incentives programme and clear career development. You will receive full training on the products you will be specialising in, and you will have access to a world leading catalogue of technology-based learning.


If this sounds like the opportunity for you then please apply by sending a copy of your most recent CV.

companyAddresses:
3351 Michelson Drive, Suite 100.
Irvine, CA 92612 USA
companyDescription:
Ingram Micro helps businesses Realize the Promise of Technology™. 
It delivers a full spectrum of global technology and supply chain services to businesses around the world. Deep expertise in technology solutions, mobility, cloud, and supply chain solutions enables its business partners to operate efficiently and successfully in the markets they serve. 
Unrivaled agility, deep market insights and the trust and dependability that come from decades of proven relationships, set Ingram Micro apart and ahead. More at www.ingrammicro.com
companyBriefDescription: Headquartered in Irvine, California, Ingram Micro is the world's largest distributor of wholesale technology products.

title: Business Development Executive
descriptionHtml:
Business Development Executive

About Us

Ingram Micro touches 90% of the world’s technology. We’re here to help businesses discover solutions that simplify the complicated world of tech - enabling them to operate more efficiently and successfully in the markets they serve.


Ingram Micro is the business behind the world’s brands. We are proud to be partnered with some of the best-in-class manufacturers within tech; Apple, Cisco, Dell, HP, Aruba, Microsoft, Dell EMC, AWS,

Be your best, working with the best.

About the Job

An exciting opportunity has arisen to join the Microsoft team as a Business Development Executive. You will be responsible for the overall development and execution of driving the technology area. This includes the ownership and continued development of relationships with our aligned partners and vendors, and supporting or leading the origination and/or closing of sales opportunities.

What you can expect to be getting up to as a Business Development Executive

  • Collaborate with Vendor Channel managers in the region to build a successful partnership to grow revenue in UK/I
  • Build long-lasting relationships with senior executives at their organizations.
  • Develop joint go-to-market and business plans and execute against them by reporting on progress vs plan.
  • Work closely with highly skilled Sales and Commercial managers to support the strategic growth plan for the category.
  • Manage day-to-day support and enquiries from Channel Partners. Act as the voice of partners within the organization.
  • Lead regular business reviews with partners and offer a strategic and analytical perspective on the partnership, identifying areas of improvement and growth.
  • Manage day to day support and enquiries from partners
  • Ensure partners are trained/educated on the vendor value proposition

In order to set you up for success, we are looking for the following skills and experience

  • A strong relationship builder with the ability to manage existing accounts along with developing new business with target accounts.
  • Strong business development background
  • Ability to work independently and within a wider team
  • Financial analytics and numeracy skills
  • Excellent communication, presentation and negotiating skills
  • Ability to plan and organise all aspects of the role with proven experience in business planning.

We Are Ingram Micro
Ingram Micro is a leading technology company for the global information technology ecosystem. With the ability to reach nearly 90% of the global population, we play a vital role in the worldwide IT sales channel, bringing products and services from technology manufacturers and cloud providers to business-to-business technology experts. Through Ingram Micro Xvantage™, our AI-powered digital platform, we offer what we believe to be the industry’s first business-to- consumer-like experience. We also provide a broad range of technology services, including financing, specialized marketing, and lifecycle management, as well as technical pre- and post- sales professional support. Learn more at www.ingrammicro.com.

Make an application to join the team!
None of this is achievable without great people, with a complete customer focus.

Our team is as much about our people as it is our customers and business partners. We want associates with a strong desire to succeed. We offer a competitive base, commission, market leading incentives programme and clear career development. You will receive full training on the products you will be specialising in, and you will have access to a world leading catalogue of technology-based learning.
If this sounds like the opportunity for you then please apply by sending a copy of your most recent CV.

title: Senior Business Development Representative (Life Sciences)
descriptionHtml:
Senior Business Development Representative (Life Sciences)

We have an opportunity for a Senior Business Development Representative (Life Sciences) to join us at Honeywell, in the UK , where you will impact the company's revenue growth and success in the market. By driving qualified leads and pipeline growth, you will contribute to the company's revenue generation and business growth. Your ability to optimise lead generation campaigns and programs will help expand the customer base and increase market share, positioning Honeywell as a leader in the industry.

This is a remote role.

Honeywell

We have been innovating for more than 100 years and now we’re creating what’s next. Our programs serve a global community and our hometowns too. We provide resources and financial support to causes around the world and encourage employee volunteerism. We are committed to the highest standard in everything we do!

Key Responsibilities

  • Drive qualified pipeline growth through targeted demand‑generation strategies and optimised lead‑generation campaigns.
  • Analyse and manage lead data to improve conversion rates, lead quality, and overall funnel performance.
  • Conduct market, company, and contact research to uncover new sales opportunities and support relationship development.
  • Create effective prospecting scripts, qualify leads using BANT, and maintain accurate account data in Salesforce.
    • Partner closely with Sales teams to nurture prospects, share insights, and support Territory and Opportunity planning.

Key Skills and Qualifications

  • Minimum of 2 years of experience in Sales Development, Field Sales, Marketing or adjacent roles.
  • Proven track record of driving lead generation and pipeline growth.
  • Strong understanding of marketing automation and CRM tools.
    • Excellent analytical and data-driven mindset.

We Value

  • Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or a related field.
  • Experience in Life Sciences related industry, such as pharmaceuticals, biotechnology, genomics, and food & beverage
  • Experience in technology or enterprise software, especially SaaS
    • Excellent client facing skills with ability to engage with C-suite & senior executives

Our Offer

  • A culture that fosters inclusion, diversity, and innovation in an international work environment
  • Market specific training and ongoing personal development.
    • Experienced leaders to support your professional development

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

Join us now and be part of a global team of thinkers, innovators, dreamers, and doers who make the things that make the future!

#TheFutureIsWhatWeMakeIt


Honeywell helps organizations solve the world's most complex challenges in automation, the future of aviation and energy transition. As a trusted partner, we provide actionable solutions and innovation through our Aerospace Technologies, Building Automation, Energy and Sustainability Solutions, and Industrial Automation business segments – powered by our Honeywell Forge software – that help make the world smarter, safer and more sustainable.

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

companyDescription: Whether you are a supply chain expert, software engineer, engineer, customer service agent, salesperson or a finance professional, we have your dream job.
companyBriefDescription: Be a #futureshaper and help change the way the world works.

title: Business Development Executive
descriptionHtml:
Job Advert

Southeast
Basic rate DOE - uncapped commission
  • Mon – Fri, 8:00 am – 6:00 pm, 30 min unpaid break - 45hr week (open to flexible working discussions)
Vehicle allowance, open to flexible working, birthday leave, work mobile phone and laptop.

This role is field-based.

Your Role

If you are looking to be part of an award-winning company offering you a great career, then look no further! At Rygor Auto, they are looking for a motivated and driven business development executive in van sales to join their award-winning company. You will be passionate about delivering exceptional customer service and working with customers to provide van solutions to really meet their business needs.

This role is all about finding new business from the first door knock to closing the deal and fostering long-term relationships with fleet customers. You will be able to identify the right van requirements for the customer and present them with solutions, and if you have experience and interest in EV (electric vehicles), that is definitely advantageous.

You are eager to find new business and know how to close the deal. They are happy to talk about flexible working, so do not hesitate to get in touch if you are looking for a rewarding and lucrative sales career.

Key Skills & Behaviours for a Van Sales Role

  • Prospecting & Lead Generation: Confident in identifying new opportunities, approaching potential customers, and building a strong sales pipeline.
  • Resilience: Able to stay motivated and positive, even during challenging periods or when targets are tough.
  • Handling Rejection: Maintains professionalism, learns from setbacks, and quickly resets to move on to the next opportunity using what you have learned from your experiences to improve.
  • Customer Relationship Building: Skilled at establishing trust, understanding customer needs, and nurturing long-term relationships.
  • Strong Product Knowledge: Keen to understand vehicle features, benefits, and spec options to confidently present tailored solutions.
  • Communication & Influencing Skills: Able to clearly explain value, negotiate effectively, and close deals with confidence.
  • Organisation & Time Management: Manages appointments, follow-ups, and admin efficiently to maximise selling time.
  • Self‑Motivation & Drive: Results-focused, target-driven, and proactive in taking ownership and accountability of personal and site performance.
  • Adaptability: Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment where no two days are the same.
  • Professionalism & Integrity: Always represents the company positively and maintains high ethical standards in line with our values of being Rewarding, Reliable and Respectful.

‍ Day to day, you will be:

  • Researching assigned areas to identify new business opportunities, utilising your resilience and perseverance to book in sales meetings and close the deal with new customers as well as existing opportunities.
  • Promptly answering phone calls and assisting walk-ins and wholesale customers with their needs when you work at a site, ensuring no opportunity is left unexplored.
  • Establishing and fostering positive relationships with other departments, especially after-sales, to ensure a seamless and brilliant experience for our customers.
  • Concluding sales contracts and making required amendments, always ensuring accuracy and efficiency.
  • Customer database management – it is essential to keep your customer records up to date to ensure best practice and customer communication.
  • Promoting current sales campaigns, achieving sales targets.
  • First-class vehicle handovers and recording customer satisfaction.
  • Oversee the entire sales process, beginning from lead generation till closing deals – making sure you close the loop.
  • Develop business strategies that help the organisation in increasing sales and lead to growth.

DBS: Please be advised that all successful applicants to Rygor or any of our associated businesses are required to undertake a DBS check prior to joining the business.

Benefits for you

  • An extra day off for your Birthday
  • 23 days of annual leave per year PLUS Bank Holidays
  • Four times death in service benefit.
  • Internal Mentorship programme
  • Working for WhatVan?’s ‘Van Dealer of the Year 2026’.
  • Working for Motor Trader’s ‘Employer of the Year’.
  • Working for an official Great Place to Work™️
  • Working for a workplace which champions women.
  • Awarded Armed Forces Covenant Silver Award (RLS)
  • Working with their award-winning team – several of our team were recognised at the MT WIMT awards, and they won silver at the internal communication and engagement awards for our new starter onboarding process.
  • Auto-enrolment pension.
  • Self-development journal
  • Rygor Group Ranked 101 in SW Business Insider’s Top 500 Companies
  • Enhanced Maternity and Paternity pay policies and return to work post mat-leave policy.
  • Access to their wellness programme and Employee Assistance Programmes, including WeCare from Canada Life (EAP and 24HR Online GP access and second Medical Opinion).
  • Mental Health First Aiders.
  • Reward and recognition programmes, including our annual Rygor Group Awards and opportunities to attend the Rygor Wembley box as a celebration of your hard work and dedication.
  • Annual appraisals programme and progression opportunities.
  • Manufacturer training and access to our in-house training.
  • Long service recognition and annual leave increase after 5 years’ service.
  • Rygor Auto and Rygor Service Solutions are Maintenance Provision Rating Scheme (MPRS) accredited
  • Branded uniform
  • Charities of the Year programme, Army Sport sponsors and community engagement initiatives


 About Rygor Group

Rygor are an award-winning, multi-franchise Dealer Group with branches across England, offering extensive coverage for customers throughout the M5, M4 and M42 corridors. Their business has gone from strength to strength in recent years, with significant investment in site renovations nationwide and exciting expansions, including the launch of a brand-new Mercedes-Benz Passenger Car Aftersales facility in 2024.

Their commitment to excellence has earned them multiple industry and network awards, and in 2025, they were proud to be named Employer of the Year at the prestigious Motor Trader Awards. They also received the Silver award for their onboarding experience at the ICE awards, meaning that if you're considering a new career with Rygor, you can be confident you'll receive an award-winning induction and support from day one.

They are officially a Great Place to Work™ (multiple years running), and their company culture truly reflects their core values: Rewarding, Reliable, and Respectful. Exceptional customer service is at the heart of what they do and so is creating an environment where their team can thrive.

Rygor Group comprises a diverse collection of UK automotive businesses, with a primary focus on trucks, vans, coaches, passenger cars, armoured vehicles, body repairs and coachbuilding. Through the hard work of their team members, they have secured their position as one of the UK’s top automotive Groups, with a turnover of circa £300m a year.


Our Process

When applying, please include a full current CV. Once this has been reviewed, we will be in touch to advise if your application will be progressed through to the next stage. The Riverside Resourcing & Consultancy recruitment process reflects our obligations under relevant UK legislation. Whilst all applications are carefully considered, we are only able to progress applications from candidates who have the right to work in the United Kingdom.

Managing your data

We care about your personal data. The General Data Protection Regulation provides you with more control, and by applying to us, we consider this to be an agreement to process your personal data in support of our recruitment and selection process. Your application data is held within our secure applicant tracking system through hireful - you will be given access to a candidate zone in this system where you can monitor and manage your application and see status updates. We will communicate with you, by email or phone, throughout the recruitment process so that we can keep you updated and inform you of your application progress.
descriptionText:
Job Advert

 Southeast
 Basic rate DOE - uncapped commission
 Mon – Fri, 8:00 am – 6:00 pm, 30 min unpaid break - 45hr week (open to flexible working discussions)
 Vehicle allowance, open to flexible working, birthday leave, work mobile phone and laptop.

This role is field-based.

 Your Role

If you are looking to be part of an award-winning company offering you a great career, then look no further! At Rygor Auto, they are looking for a motivated and driven business development executive in van sales to join their award-winning company. You will be passionate about delivering exceptional customer service and working with customers to provide van solutions to really meet their business needs.

This role is all about finding new business from the first door knock to closing the deal and fostering long-term relationships with fleet customers. You will be able to identify the right van requirements for the customer and present them with solutions, and if you have experience and interest in EV (electric vehicles), that is definitely advantageous.

You are eager to find new business and know how to close the deal. They are happy to talk about flexible working, so do not hesitate to get in touch if you are looking for a rewarding and lucrative sales career.

Key Skills & Behaviours for a Van Sales Role

Prospecting & Lead Generation: Confident in identifying new opportunities, approaching potential customers, and building a strong sales pipeline.
Resilience: Able to stay motivated and positive, even during challenging periods or when targets are tough.
Handling Rejection: Maintains professionalism, learns from setbacks, and quickly resets to move on to the next opportunity using what you have learned from your experiences to improve.
Customer Relationship Building: Skilled at establishing trust, understanding customer needs, and nurturing long-term relationships.
Strong Product Knowledge: Keen to understand vehicle features, benefits, and spec options to confidently present tailored solutions.
Communication & Influencing Skills: Able to clearly explain value, negotiate effectively, and close deals with confidence.
Organisation & Time Management: Manages appointments, follow-ups, and admin efficiently to maximise selling time.
Self‑Motivation & Drive: Results-focused, target-driven, and proactive in taking ownership and accountability of personal and site performance.
Adaptability: Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment where no two days are the same.
Professionalism & Integrity: Always represents the company positively and maintains high ethical standards in line with our values of being Rewarding, Reliable and Respectful.

‍ Day to day, you will be:

Researching assigned areas to identify new business opportunities, utilising your resilience and perseverance to book in sales meetings and close the deal with new customers as well as existing opportunities.
Promptly answering phone calls and assisting walk-ins and wholesale customers with their needs when you work at a site, ensuring no opportunity is left unexplored.
Establishing and fostering positive relationships with other departments, especially after-sales, to ensure a seamless and brilliant experience for our customers.
Concluding sales contracts and making required amendments, always ensuring accuracy and efficiency.
Customer database management – it is essential to keep your customer records up to date to ensure best practice and customer communication.
Promoting current sales campaigns, achieving sales targets.
First-class vehicle handovers and recording customer satisfaction.
Oversee the entire sales process, beginning from lead generation till closing deals – making sure you close the loop.
Develop business strategies that help the organisation in increasing sales and lead to growth.

DBS: Please be advised that all successful applicants to Rygor or any of our associated businesses are required to undertake a DBS check prior to joining the business.

 Benefits for you

An extra day off for your Birthday
23 days of annual leave per year PLUS Bank Holidays
Four times death in service benefit.
Internal Mentorship programme
Working for WhatVan?’s ‘Van Dealer of the Year 2026’.
Working for Motor Trader’s ‘Employer of the Year’.
Working for an official Great Place to Work™️
Working for a workplace which champions women.
Awarded Armed Forces Covenant Silver Award (RLS)
Working with their award-winning team – several of our team were recognised at the MT WIMT awards, and they won silver at the internal communication and engagement awards for our new starter onboarding process.
Auto-enrolment pension.
Self-development journal
Rygor Group Ranked 101 in SW Business Insider’s Top 500 Companies
Enhanced Maternity and Paternity pay policies and return to work post mat-leave policy.
Access to their wellness programme and Employee Assistance Programmes, including WeCare from Canada Life (EAP and 24HR Online GP access and second Medical Opinion).
Mental Health First Aiders.
Reward and recognition programmes, including our annual Rygor Group Awards and opportunities to attend the Rygor Wembley box as a celebration of your hard work and dedication.
Annual appraisals programme and progression opportunities.
Manufacturer training and access to our in-house training.
Long service recognition and annual leave increase after 5 years’ service.
Rygor Auto and Rygor Service Solutions are Maintenance Provision Rating Scheme (MPRS) accredited
Branded uniform
Charities of the Year programme, Army Sport sponsors and community engagement initiatives

 About Rygor Group

Rygor are an award-winning, multi-franchise Dealer Group with branches across England, offering extensive coverage for customers throughout the M5, M4 and M42 corridors. Their business has gone from strength to strength in recent years, with significant investment in site renovations nationwide and exciting expansions, including the launch of a brand-new Mercedes-Benz Passenger Car Aftersales facility in 2024.

Their commitment to excellence has earned them multiple industry and network awards, and in 2025, they were proud to be named Employer of the Year at the prestigious Motor Trader Awards. They also received the Silver award for their onboarding experience at the ICE awards, meaning that if you're considering a new career with Rygor, you can be confident you'll receive an award-winning induction and support from day one.

They are officially a Great Place to Work™ (multiple years running), and their company culture truly reflects their core values: Rewarding, Reliable, and Respectful. Exceptional customer service is at the heart of what they do and so is creating an environment where their team can thrive.

Rygor Group comprises a diverse collection of UK automotive businesses, with a primary focus on trucks, vans, coaches, passenger cars, armoured vehicles, body repairs and coachbuilding. Through the hard work of their team members, they have secured their position as one of the UK’s top automotive Groups, with a turnover of circa £300m a year.

Our Process

When applying, please include a full current CV. Once this has been reviewed, we will be in touch to advise if your application will be progressed through to the next stage. The Riverside Resourcing & Consultancy recruitment process reflects our obligations under relevant UK legislation. Whilst all applications are carefully considered, we are only able to progress applications from candidates who have the right to work in the United Kingdom.

Managing your data

We care about your personal data. The General Data Protection Regulation provides you with more control, and by applying to us, we consider this to be an agreement to process your personal data in support of our recruitment and selection process. Your application data is held within our secure applicant tracking system through hireful - you will be given access to a candidate zone in this system where you can monitor and manage your application and see status updates. We will communicate with you, by email or phone, throughout the recruitment process so that we can keep you updated and inform you of your application progress.
